title: Argentinian Cocoa Bake
description: A classic Argentine dessert featuring a rich cocoa sauce and a light, flaky crust. Perfect for a sweet treat after dinner.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and melted butter.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and whisk until just combined.
Pour the batter into a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth the top.
Place the baking dish on the preheated grill and bake for 25 minutes.
While the cake is baking, prepare the sauce by whisking together the cocoa powder, sugar, and 1 cup of hot water in a small saucepan.
Bring the sauce to a boil over medium heat, whisking constantly.
Remove the cake from the oven and pour the sauce over the top.
Return the cake to the grill and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbly and the cake is set.
Remove the cake from the grill and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
